summary: Critically audited DinoBrain against verified RAG knowledge and the user's desired OS using local code/data verification plus 10 subagent reviews. Produced and pushed a consensus audit report identifying P0/P1 improvements: outer wrapper fail-closed, real source/provenance lineage, true read-only/review mode, protected installer updates, real hybrid retrieval, lower-noise context eval, behavior eval, lifecycle backpressure, durable SQLite/cold memory, safer public sync, task-correlated Observatory, and release/version parity.
decisions: DinoBrain currently has a credible skeleton but does not yet satisfy the user's desired closed-loop LLM Wiki OS guarantees.
Treat hook wrapper fail-open paths, source lineage resolution, read-only/review semantics, and protected installer update behavior as P0 work before claiming OS completion.

Treat real dense hybrid retrieval, context/behavior evaluation, lifecycle backpressure, durable SQLite refresh, public/private sync split, Observatory read-trace correlation, and release parity as P1 work.
Do not claim lexical_fallback_v2 is real dense hybrid retrieval.
Do not claim graph node/edge count proves source-truth memory until accepted claims resolve to source chunks and provenance records.
next_steps: Implement P0-1 hook wrapper fail-closed and verifier cases.
Implement P0-2 provenance lineage resolver and graph-health real-vault gate.
Implement P0-3 read-only/review mode that blocks auto-growth, auto-compound, and auto-sync.
Implement P0-4 installer update protection for dirty/ahead/diverged data repos.
Then proceed through retrieval/eval/lifecycle/Observatory/release parity P1 roadmap in the audit report.
search_queries: DinoBrain RAG verified knowledge fail closed provenance behavior eval lifecycle observability installer recovery
verified RAG hybrid retrieval source chunk claim provenance behavior evaluation
